annotations.label: Abelian variety

anchor.snippet: an abelian variety is a smooth projective algebraic variety that is also an algebraic group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no bespoke `AbelianVariety` definition; the standard combination of `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, and `GeometricallyIntegral` over a field serves as a proxy used in the commutativity theorem.

annotations.label: Group law is commutative and variety non-singular

anchor.snippet: The group law of an abelian variety is necessarily commutative and the variety is non-singular

annotations.note: Commutativity is established for any proper geometrically integral group scheme over a field, generalising the abelian-variety case; smoothness is separately handled by `smooth_of_grpObj` in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.

annotations.label: Elliptic curve as abelian variety of dimension 1

anchor.snippet: An elliptic curve is an abelian variety of dimension 1.

annotations.note: Elliptic curves are formalized (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.EllipticCurve.*`) but no result identifies them as abelian varieties of dimension one.

annotations.label: Kodaira dimension 0

anchor.snippet: Abelian varieties have Kodaira dimension 0.

annotations.note: Kodaira dimension is not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex torus of dimension g

anchor.snippet: A complex torus of dimension g is a torus of real dimension 2 g that carries the structure of a complex manifold

annotations.note: No `ComplexTorus` declaration exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex abelian variety

anchor.snippet: A complex abelian variety of dimension g is a complex torus of dimension g that is also a projective algebraic variety over the field of complex numbers.

annotations.note: No notion of complex abelian variety is formalized.

annotations.label: Morphism of abelian varieties

anchor.snippet: A morphism of abelian varieties is a morphism of the underlying algebraic varieties that preserves the identity element for the group structure.

annotations.note: No abelian-variety morphism type exists; only morphisms of monoid objects in `Over (Spec k)` are available generically.

annotations.label: Isogeny

anchor.snippet: An isogeny is a finite-to-one morphism.

annotations.note: There is no `Isogeny` declaration anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of algebraic structure on complex torus

anchor.snippet: When a complex torus carries the structure of an algebraic variety, this structure is necessarily unique.

annotations.note: Not formalized — no infrastructure for complex tori or GAGA-style uniqueness exists for abelian varieties.

annotations.label: Riemann criterion for abelian variety

anchor.snippet: Then X is an abelian variety if and only if there exists a positive definite hermitian form on V whose imaginary part takes integral values

annotations.note: The Riemann bilinear/period relations characterising abelian varieties are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemann form

anchor.snippet: Such a form on X is usually called a (non-degenerate) Riemann form

annotations.note: No `RiemannForm` declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobian variety of a curve

anchor.section: The Jacobian of an algebraic curve
anchor.snippet: Every algebraic curve C of genus

annotations.note: Mathlib's uses of `Jacobian` refer to either analytic Jacobians or Jacobian coordinates on elliptic curves, not to the Jacobian variety of a curve.

annotations.label: Jacobian variety

anchor.section: The Jacobian of an algebraic curve
anchor.snippet: The abelian variety J is called the Jacobian variety of C , for any non-singular curve C over the complex numbers.

annotations.note: No Jacobian-variety construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian function

anchor.snippet: An abelian function is a meromorphic function on an abelian variety

annotations.note: Not formalized; meromorphic-function infrastructure on abelian varieties is absent.

annotations.label: Matsusaka's theorem

anchor.snippet: One important structure theorem of abelian varieties is Matsusaka's theorem

annotations.note: No occurrence of Matsusaka in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic definition of abelian variety

anchor.snippet: Two equivalent definitions of abelian variety over a general field k are commonly in use

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the combination `GrpObj G ∧ IsProper G.hom ∧ GeometricallyIntegral G.hom` over `Spec k` in lieu of an explicit `AbelianVariety` predicate.

annotations.label: Group of points is commutative

anchor.section: Structure of the group of points
anchor.snippet: Its group of points can be proven to be commutative

annotations.note: Proper geometrically integral group schemes over a field are shown to be commutative monoid objects, which yields commutativity on points.

annotations.label: Torsion group structure in characteristic zero

anchor.section: Structure of the group of points
anchor.snippet: the torsion group of an abelian variety of dimension g is isomorphic to

annotations.note: Not formalized; no result describing `(ℚ/ℤ)^{2g}` torsion of an abelian variety exists.

annotations.label: n-torsion in characteristic p

anchor.section: Structure of the group of points
anchor.snippet: When the base field is an algebraically closed field of characteristic p , the n -torsion is still isomorphic to

annotations.note: No formalisation of n-torsion of abelian varieties in characteristic p.

annotations.label: Mordell–Weil theorem

anchor.section: Structure of the group of points
anchor.snippet: The group of k -rational points for a global field k is finitely generated by the Mordell-Weil theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the abstract descent step (`CommGroup.fg_of_descent'` / `AddCommGroup.fg_of_descent'`) that feeds into the standard proof of Mordell–Weil, but not the theorem itself.

annotations.label: Rank of an abelian variety

anchor.section: Structure of the group of points
anchor.snippet: a finite commutative group for some non-negative integer r called the rank of the abelian variety

annotations.note: No rank invariant for abelian varieties is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Product of abelian varieties

anchor.snippet: The product of an abelian variety A of dimension m , and an abelian variety B of dimension n , over the same field, is an abelian variety

annotations.note: Not formalized as a statement about abelian varieties; only generic products in `Scheme` / `Over (Spec k)` are available.

annotations.label: Simple abelian variety

anchor.snippet: An abelian variety is simple if it is not isogenous to a product of abelian varieties of lower dimension.

annotations.note: No notion of simple abelian variety in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decomposition into simple abelian varieties

anchor.snippet: Any abelian variety is isogenous to a product of simple abelian varieties.

annotations.note: Poincaré complete reducibility is not formalised.

annotations.label: Dual abelian variety

anchor.snippet: To an abelian variety A over a field k , one associates a dual abelian variety

annotations.note: No dual abelian variety construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Family of degree 0 line bundles

anchor.snippet: A family of degree 0 line bundles parametrised by a k -variety T is defined to be a line bundle L on

annotations.note: No Picard scheme / degree-zero line-bundle family infrastructure for abelian varieties exists.

annotations.label: Poincaré bundle universal property

anchor.snippet: Then there is a variety

annotations.note: The Poincaré bundle is not formalised.

annotations.label: Duality is contravariant functorial

anchor.snippet: This association is a duality in the sense that it is contravariant functorial

annotations.note: Not formalised; dual abelian variety absent.

annotations.label: Cartier duality of n-torsion

anchor.snippet: the n -torsion group schemes of dual abelian varieties are Cartier duals of each other

annotations.note: Cartier duality of finite flat group schemes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polarisation

anchor.snippet: A polarisation of an abelian variety is an isogeny from an abelian variety to its dual that is symmetric with respect to double-duality

annotations.note: No polarisation of abelian varieties is defined; the `polarisation`/`polarization` hits in Mathlib refer to bilinear forms and root systems.

annotations.label: Polarised abelian varieties have finite automorphism groups

anchor.snippet: Polarised abelian varieties have finite automorphism groups

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Principal polarisation

anchor.snippet: A principal polarisation is a polarisation that is an isomorphism.

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Jacobians and principal polarisation

anchor.snippet: Jacobians of curves are naturally equipped with a principal polarisation as soon as one picks an arbitrary rational base point

annotations.note: Not formalised — Jacobian variety and polarisations are both missing.

annotations.label: Rosati involution

anchor.snippet: A polarisation induces a Rosati involution on the endomorphism ring

annotations.note: No `Rosati` declaration anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polarisation over complex numbers

anchor.snippet: Over the complex numbers, a polarised abelian variety can be defined as an abelian variety A together with a choice of a Riemann form H

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Equivalent Riemann forms

anchor.snippet: Two Riemann forms

annotations.note: Not formalised; no Riemann-form infrastructure.

annotations.label: Morphism of polarised abelian varieties

anchor.snippet: A morphism of polarised abelian varieties is a morphism

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Abelian scheme

anchor.snippet: An abelian scheme over a base scheme S of relative dimension g is a proper , smooth group scheme over S whose geometric fibers are connected and of dimension g

annotations.note: No `AbelianScheme` class in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-torsion as finite flat group scheme

anchor.snippet: the group of n -torsion points forms a finite flat group scheme

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Serre–Tate theorem

anchor.snippet: Deformations of abelian schemes are, according to the Serre–Tate theorem , governed by the deformation properties of the associated p -divisible groups.

annotations.note: No Serre–Tate result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian scheme from cubic with nonzero discriminant

anchor.snippet: has no repeated complex roots

annotations.note: Not formalised as an abelian-scheme example.

annotations.label: Abrashkin–Fontaine non-existence over Z

anchor.snippet: independently proved that there are no nonzero abelian varieties over

annotations.note: Not formalised.

annotations.label: Semiabelian variety

anchor.snippet: A semiabelian variety is a commutative group variety which is an extension of an abelian variety by a torus

annotations.note: No semiabelian variety definition in Mathlib.
